142.reason: [The while condition can cause an infinite loop if there is an odd number of backslashes before the closing quote, and also leads to double appending of the closing quote when the previous character is a backslash. Additionally, there is no bounds check; if the string never ends, an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException will occur.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  [The while condition can cause an infinite loop if there is an odd number of backslashes before the closing quote, and also leads to double appending of the closing quote when the previous character is a backslash. Additionally, there is no bounds check; if the string never ends, an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException will occur.]

156.reason: [Same issue as above: the while condition is flawed for escaped quotes and lacks bounds checking.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as above: the while condition is flawed for escaped quotes and lacks bounds checking.]

78.reason: [The loop condition does not check for end-of-input, causing potential StringIndexOutOfBoundsException when the string literal is not properly terminated.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  [The loop condition does not check for end-of-input, causing potential StringIndexOutOfBoundsException when the string literal is not properly terminated.]

87.reason: [Same issue as string literal: no bounds check, may read beyond input length.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as string literal: no bounds check, may read beyond input length.]

3437.reason: The logic is inverted. It should return !errors.isEmpty() because hasError() should be true when there ARE errors (list is not empty). Currently, it returns true for correct programs (empty list), causing the compiler to skip generating parser.txt.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The logic is inverted. It should return !errors.isEmpty() because hasError() should be true when there ARE errors (list is not empty). Currently, it returns true for correct programs (empty list), causing the compiler to skip generating parser.txt.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The logic is inverted. isEmpty() returns true when there are NO errors. This causes the compiler to think there are errors and write to error.txt (likely empty) instead of parser.txt. It should be !errors.isEmpty().
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [This returns true when there are NO errors, but it should return true when there ARE errors. The logic is inverted - it should be "!errors.isEmpty()"]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [This returns true when there are NO errors, but it should return true when there ARE errors. The logic is inverted - it should be "!errors.isEmpty()" or "errors.size() > 0"]
